metric_value: $1.2 billion

metric_value: $500-600 million annually

metric_value: 5% planning full replacement

metric_value: 65% of enterprise customer base

metric_name: Customers using HP 3000 as central production with surrounding systems
metric_value: 30% of enterprise customer base

metric_value: Customer First (installed-base retention; not new customer acquisition)
notes: HP formally adopted Customer First strategy end of 1995

metric_value: New customer acquisition platform; Unix/RDBMS focused
notes: HP 9000 surpassed HP 3000 revenues in 1993

metric_value: Continuous customer feedback driving product development
notes: Five-pillar implementation framework for Customer First strategy

metric_value: Customer feedback guiding ISV application partnering
notes: Commercial Systems Division representing all HP 3000 customers to ISVs

metric_value: Direct HP corporate customer dialogue bypassing traditional sales channels
notes: 1-800-224-HP3K hotline; HP Adviser newsletter; HP Chronicle

metric_value: Open interfaces and interoperability with Unix, NT, MVS ahead of demand
notes: Oracle Transparent Gateway for IMAGE/SQL cited as example

metric_value: Improving HP 3000 OS to match best-in-class Unix and NT alternatives
notes: Continuous functional parity goal with alternative platforms

metric_value: Over 30 models; 918LX at $26,000 to 996/800 SMP at $720,000
notes: Range supports <64 users to 1,300 concurrent users

metric_name: PA-8000 upgrade impact on HP 3000 high-end
metric_value: ~2x performance improvement at 10% price premium by end of 1996
notes: Aberdeen projection for 996 and 969 class systems

metric_name: PA-7200 upgrade impact on HP 3000 low-end
metric_value: 30-35% performance improvement; no price change
notes: Applies to 9X8LX/RX systems

metric_name: HP 3000 key differentiator vs Unix alternatives
metric_value: Integrated MPE/iX + IMAGE/SQL architecture; best-in-class reliability and availability
notes: Users cite 24x7 reliability for global operations as primary loyalty driver

metric_value: Not cost-effective for component-based systems with portable ISV RDBMS
notes: Paradox: integrated architecture strength also limits extensibility

metric_value: HP will support HP 3000 customers for decades; Customer First ensures continued viability
notes: Aberdeen explicitly predicts HP will not discontinue or starve-and-harvest the HP 3000

metric_name: HP 3000 transition to HP-Intel Merced architecture
metric_value: By year 2000 HP will design Merced processors into HP 3000 and rewrite MPE/iX
notes: Aberdeen projects MPE/iX rewrite for Merced chip by 2000

metric_value: HP can count on stable customer demand for a long time into the future
notes: Based on 95% customer retention data

metric_value: HP announced end-of-life November 2001; support extended to December 31 2010
notes: HP kept platform alive 5+ years beyond prediction period; ultimately discontinued. Source: HP official announcement and Wikipedia HP 3000 article.

metric_value: MPE/iX was never ported to Itanium/Merced; HP 3000 EOL announced instead
notes: The Merced/Itanium transition did not include HP 3000; prediction proved incorrect.

metric_name: HP 3000 customer base continuity actual outcome
metric_value: Substantial portion of installed base continued using HP 3000 through 2010 EOL; third-party support ongoing post-2010
notes: Customer loyalty prediction proved largely correct; some customers still running HP 3000 as of mid-2020s on third-party support.

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of Customer First strategy
metric_value: Realistic and pragmatic; correctly positions HP 3000 among its own product family
notes: Aberdeen endorses strategy as well-aligned with market realities

metric_value: User-based pricing cuts up to 40% on low-end systems
notes: Change from processor-based pricing; Commercial Systems Division working with ISVs to adopt same model

metric_value: Over $6 billion
notes: HP General Systems Division revenue cited

metric_value: 996/800 8-way SMP supports up to 1,300 concurrent users at $720,000 base
